name: Optimize wjson performance across Lua versions

description: Baseline performance (original wjson)

asi.hypothesis: baseline
asi.notes: total_ms = sum of encode+decode across 8 benchmark scenarios (4 synthetic, 4 real datasets) for LuaJIT. Other Lua versions tracked for regression.

description: Manual escape_string for all Lua versions: LuaJIT encode -49% but PUC Lua +34-48% regression

asi.hypothesis: Replace str_gsub with manual byte scanning for string escaping
asi.result: LuaJIT encode nearly 2x faster but PUC Lua catastrophically slower
asi.key_insight: Manual byte scanning is fast on LuaJIT (JIT-compiled) but slow on PUC Lua (interpreted). str_gsub is C-optimized in PUC Lua and hard to beat.
asi.next_action: Use conditional: escape_string for LuaJIT, str_gsub for PUC Lua

description: Conditional escape_string: LuaJIT manual scanning, PUC Lua str_gsub. LuaJIT encode -55%, no PUC regression

asi.hypothesis: Conditional escape: manual byte scanning for LuaJIT, str_gsub for PUC Lua
asi.key_insight: LuaJIT JIT-compiles the manual byte scanning loop making it ~2x faster than str_gsub. PUC Lua benefits slightly from the ESCAPE_PATTERN local variable (avoids recompiling pattern each call).
asi.result: LuaJIT encode -55%, PUC Lua encode slightly better too

description: Direct integer parsing: compute from bytes instead of tonumber(str_sub). LuaJIT decode -20%

asi.hypothesis: Direct integer computation in parse_number instead of tonumber(str_sub())
asi.result: LuaJIT decode -20%, encode also improved. PUC Lua roughly unchanged.
asi.key_insight: Avoiding str_sub+tonumber for integers is a big win for LuaJIT. The JIT compiles the arithmetic loop. PUC Lua doesn't benefit much since it falls through to the same slow path for non-integers.
asi.note: The encode improvement is unexpected - might be noise or side effect of code layout

description: Move depth check to array/object branches only — worse performance

asi.hypothesis: Move depth check from decode_value entry to only array/object branches
asi.result: Worse: 214.89 vs 169.85. The extra branch instructions hurt more than the saved comparison.
asi.key_insight: A single depth > 20 check at function entry is cheaper than two conditional checks inside branches. The JIT handles the simple early-exit pattern better.

description: Extend SMALL_INTS from 0-9 to 0-99, modest improvement across all versions

asi.hypothesis: Extend SMALL_INTS cache from 0-9 to 0-99
asi.result: Small improvement across all versions. LuaJIT encode 88->87, decode 82->81. PUC Lua also slightly better.
asi.key_insight: Caching integer string representations avoids tostring() calls for two-digit integers. Modest but consistent improvement across all Lua versions.

description: Extend SMALL_INTS to 0-999 — worse, table too large

asi.hypothesis: Extend SMALL_INTS from 0-99 to 0-999
asi.result: Worse: 199 vs 168. Larger table hurts cache performance.
asi.key_insight: 0-99 is the sweet spot for SMALL_INTS. Beyond that, the hash table lookup cost outweighs the tostring() savings. 0-999 causes cache pressure.
asi.next_action: Try other optimizations for decode path

description: Reorder number encoding: SMALL_INTS before NaN/Infinity — worse

asi.hypothesis: Reorder number encoding: SMALL_INTS check before NaN/Infinity
asi.result: Worse: 201 vs 168. SMALL_INTS lookup for non-integer floats is wasteful.
asi.key_insight: NaN check (val ~= val) is cheap on LuaJIT. SMALL_INTS table lookup for floats wastes time since floats never hit the cache. The original order is better: NaN/Infinity first (cheap checks), then SMALL_INTS (hits for integers only).

description: Remove clear_buffer from drain_buffer — no measurable improvement

asi.hypothesis: Remove clear_buffer from drain_buffer since encode_value always overwrites entries
asi.result: No improvement: 170 vs 168 (within noise). drain_buffer is called once per encode(), not on hot path.
asi.key_insight: clear_buffer overhead is negligible since it's only called once per top-level encode, not per value. The hot path is encode_value which doesn't call drain/clear.

description: LuaJIT string.buffer encode — worse, sb:put() overhead too high

asi.hypothesis: Use LuaJIT string.buffer for encode path instead of buf[] + tbl_concat
asi.result: Worse: 183 vs 168. string.buffer sb:put() is slower than array writes for this workload.
asi.key_insight: LuaJIT JIT-compiles buf[n]=val into very fast machine code. sb:put() is a C FFI call each time, which has interop overhead. The 31x win from the microbenchmark was for repeated encode/decode of the same string (where sb:reset() is called many times). For a single encode of a large object, the overhead per put() call dominates.
asi.next_action: Try other decode optimizations, or try reducing function call overhead in other ways

description: Combine string writes into single entry — worse, string concat overhead

asi.hypothesis: Combine no-escape string writes into single buffer entry with concatenation
asi.result: Worse: 222 vs 168. String concatenation '"' .. val .. '"' creates a new string each time, which is slower than 3 separate buffer entries.
asi.key_insight: The buf[] + tbl_concat pattern works because each buffer entry is a reference to an existing string. Creating new strings via .. in the loop adds allocation overhead that outweighs the reduced buffer size. The JIT-optimized buf[n]=val writes are cheaper than string concatenation.

description: Split nil/null check — worse, extra branch overhead

asi.hypothesis: Split nil/null check into separate if blocks to avoid null comparison for nil values
asi.result: Worse: 220 vs 168. Extra branch and code bloat hurt performance.
asi.key_insight: Combined 'val == nil or val == null' is efficient because it short-circuits. For most values, both are false. The JIT handles the combined condition as efficiently as separate checks. The extra return path also hurts instruction cache.

description: Peek byte for trailing comma — worse

asi.hypothesis: Peek at next byte instead of calling skip_whitespace for trailing comma check
asi.key_insight: skip_whitespace is already very efficient. Adding a manual peek adds branching overhead.

description: Use shared_encode_buf in encode to reduce allocations

asi.hypothesis: Use shared_encode_buf for encode() to avoid table allocation per call
asi.key_insight: Pre-allocated buffer reduces GC overhead in high-frequency encode calls.

description: Avoid re-scanning digits in parse_number slow path — slight regression/noise

asi.hypothesis: Avoid re-scanning digits in parse_number slow path
asi.key_insight: The overhead of more complex logic in parse_number (extra state/branches) might outweigh the re-scanning of few digits. Tonumber() on LuaJIT is very fast.

description: Avoid re-scanning digits in parse_number slow path — slight regression/noise

asi.hypothesis: Avoid re-scanning digits in parse_number slow path
asi.key_insight: The overhead of more complex logic in parse_number (extra state/branches) might outweigh the re-scanning of few digits. Tonumber() on LuaJIT is very fast.

description: Use str_find for fast-path check in escape_string (LuaJIT) — massive regression

asi.hypothesis: Use str_find for fast-path check in escape_string
asi.key_insight: str_find with complex patterns is extremely slow in LuaJIT compared to simple manual loops.

description: Use str_sub comparison for true, false, null in decode_value — regression

asi.hypothesis: Use str_sub comparison for boolean and null literals
asi.key_insight: Manual byte checks are faster than str_sub and string comparison for small fixed-length strings in LuaJIT.

description: Use lookup table for skip_whitespace in LuaJIT — regression

asi.hypothesis: Use lookup table for skip_whitespace
asi.key_insight: Lookup table overhead (hash lookup) is more expensive than 4 boolean comparisons in a tight loop on LuaJIT.

description: Use shared table for string parts in parse_string (LuaJIT)

asi.hypothesis: Use shared table for string parts in parse_string to avoid allocation per call
asi.result: Improved total_ms from 170.69 to 165.84. Decode time significantly improved.
asi.key_insight: Avoiding table allocation in the hot parse_string loop (for strings needing escaping or UTF-8 validation) is a clear win for decode speed.

description: Use 4-byte scan in parse_string fast path (LuaJIT) — regression

asi.hypothesis: Use 4-byte scan in parse_string fast path
asi.key_insight: The overhead of str_byte(s, i, i+3) and internal branching outweighs the benefit of fewer iterations for typical JSON strings.

description: Move number check to the top of decode_value

asi.hypothesis: Move number check to the top of decode_value for faster dispatch
asi.key_insight: Numbers are very frequent in the benchmark datasets. Checking for them first reduces the average number of branches per value.

description: Use shared table for parts in escape_string (LuaJIT)

asi.hypothesis: Use shared table for parts in escape_string to avoid allocation per call
asi.key_insight: Reducing table allocations for escaped strings is a clear win for encode performance, and also helps memory pressure overall.

description: Inline UTF-8 validation branches in parse_string (LuaJIT)

asi.hypothesis: Inline UTF-8 validation branches in parse_string to avoid loop and generic logic
asi.result: Improved total_ms from 159.24 to 154.21. Decode speed significantly better.
asi.key_insight: Inlining common UTF-8 sequence lengths (2, 3, 4 bytes) removes loop overhead and branching on 'expected' length, which is a major win for strings with non-ASCII characters.

description: Remove redundant byte nil checks in parse_number loops — regression

asi.hypothesis: Remove redundant byte nil checks in parse_number loops
asi.key_insight: LuaJIT seems to prefer the explicit check or it might be a deoptimization due to changed branch predictability.

description: Extend SMALL_INTS to include -1 to -9 — regression/noise

asi.hypothesis: Extend SMALL_INTS to include small negative integers
asi.key_insight: Hash table lookup for negative numbers might be slower than the simple negative sign logic in encode_value for very small sets.

description: Increase initial table sizes in parse_array and parse_object to 32 — regression

asi.hypothesis: Increase initial table sizes to 32 to reduce resizing
asi.key_insight: The overhead of allocating larger tables for many small objects/arrays outweighs the cost of resizing for larger ones in this benchmark.

description: Move string check to the very top of decode_value — regression

asi.hypothesis: Move string check to the top of decode_value
asi.key_insight: Numbers are even more frequent or their check is cheaper, making them the better first branch.

description: Use band instead of % in UTF-8 conversion in parse_string — regression

asi.hypothesis: Use band instead of % for UTF-8 math
asi.key_insight: LuaJIT already optimizes % for constants or power-of-two, and the change might have introduced a deoptimization or just noise.

description: Remove redundant byte fallback in decode_value (safer) — regression

asi.hypothesis: Remove redundant byte fallback in decode_value
asi.key_insight: Unexpected regression from removing code - possibly affected LuaJIT's trace compilation or inlining decisions.

description: Inline string key fast path in parse_object — regression

asi.hypothesis: Inline string key fast path in parse_object
asi.key_insight: The increased code size and complexity in parse_object might have hurt LuaJIT performance more than the saved function call helped.

description: Add tight inner loop for ASCII characters in parse_string slow path — regression

asi.hypothesis: Inner tight loop for ASCII characters in parse_string slow path
asi.key_insight: The extra inner loop and branching hurt LuaJIT performance more than it helped.

description: Move number check before string check in encode_value — massive regression

asi.hypothesis: Move number check before string check in encode_value for faster dispatch
asi.key_insight: LuaJIT might have optimized the previous order better, or the frequency of types in the benchmark favors the original order more than expected. Also, changing hot function structure can trigger deoptimizations.

description: Optimize parse_array and parse_object loop: avoid redundant skip_whitespace calls. -5% total_ms, -7% decode_ms across all Lua versions.

asi.hypothesis: Avoid redundant skip_whitespace calls in array/object loops
asi.result: Consistent improvement across all Lua versions. LuaJIT decode -7%.
asi.key_insight: The loop was calling skip_whitespace twice per element (once at the top and once at the comma check). Inlining the logic and reusing results is a clear win.
